task_name: Fundamentals of Endoscopic Surgery (FES) - Educational Overview
note: This is an educational/instructional lecture presentation about FES tasks, not a performance video requiring scoring.
content_summary: Presentation by Jeffrey Marks MD, FACS on FES Hands-on Modules at Case Medical Center. Covers five FES tasks: (1) Navigation, (2) Loop Reduction, (3) Retroflexion, (4) Mucosal Evaluation, (5) Targeting. Includes introductions and demonstrations of endoscopic techniques.
reasoning: This is a teaching presentation with narration, multiple slide transitions, and introductory demonstrations of endoscopic procedural tasks. It is not a trainee or expert performing a single FLS task for evaluation. Per classification rules, instructional content with narration and overlays should not be scored.

teaching_content.presenter: Jeffrey Marks MD, FACS, Director of Surgical Endoscopy, Associate Professor, Department of Surgery, University Hospitals Case Medical Center
demonstrated_techniques: Loop snare positioning and reduction technique
teaching_content.verbal_cues: Presentation structure: introductions to each of five FES tasks with visual demonstrations of correct technique execution
teaching_content.common_errors_addressed: Not explicitly addressed in narration; demonstrations show correct technique execution without error commentary
teaching_content.fes_program_context: FES is described as a validated hands-on testing tool for flex endoscopy skills based on endoscopy 'deconstruction' into five unique skills. Testing intended for Simbionix GI Mentor II platform; training available on any VR or non-VR system.

cannot_score_because: No individual trainee performance on a single task
Educational presentation with multiple task introductions rather than single task execution
No completion time to measure (teaching content flow, not procedural timing)
No observable performance penalties (demonstrations only, not assessment)
